completed_work: Added require_admission support to latest-context helper paths.
Verified admission-gated provider prompt contains admitted L1 context and policy exclusions for unreviewed artifacts.
key_decisions: Admission gating is optional, not mandatory, preserving current ungated context behavior by default.
Unreviewed items excluded by the gate remain visible as policy exclusions for auditability.
next_actions: Decide whether to add admission verdicts for current ABS/SYNCOMP/COMP seeds or begin topic-aware routing.
open_questions: Should admission gating later become the default for selected L1 memory while remaining optional for older ABS/SYNCOMP/COMP artifacts?
risks: Strict admission gating can reduce useful context until older artifacts receive admission verdicts.
summary_text: AI-Lab completed optional admission-gated provider context. The latest-context builder can filter candidate items by admission verdict when require_admission is enabled, keeping only admission_decision admit or admit_with_warning and excluding unreviewed or excluded items with policy reasons. build_context_pack.py exposes --require-admission, and provider paths ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py now expose the same gate. Verification showed the admitted L1 context was selected while ABS-0003, SYNCOMP-0005, and COMP-0009 appeared only as policy exclusions, not selected source context. The full test suite passed and the provider-path gate was committed as 8c4c8e8. Default context assembly remains unchanged when the gate is not requested.
